2604.21769.title: Who Defines "Best"? Towards Interactive, User-Defined Evaluation of LLM Leaderboards
2604.21769.paper_summary: LLM leaderboards are widely used to compare models and guide deployment decisions. However, leaderboard rankings are shaped by evaluation priorities set by benchmark designers, rather than by the diverse goals and constraints of actual users and organizations. A single aggregate score often obscures how models behave across different prompt types and compositions. In this work, we conduct an in-depth analysis of the dataset used in the LMArena (formerly Chatbot Arena) benchmark and investigate this evaluation challenge by designing an interactive visualization interface as a design probe. Our analysis reveals that the dataset is heavily skewed toward certain topics, that model rankings vary across prompt slices, and that preference-based judgments are used in ways that blur their intended scope. Building on this analysis, we introduce a visualization interface that allows users to define their own evaluation priorities by selecting and weighting prompt slices and to explore how rankings change accordingly. A qualitative study suggests that this interactive approach improves transparency and supports more context-specific model evaluation, pointing toward alternative ways to design and use LLM leaderboards.
2604.21769.paper_summary_zh: LLM 排行榜被廣泛用來比較模型並指導部署決策。<lb/>然而，排行榜的排名是由基準設計者設定的評估優先事項所影響，而不是由實際用戶和組織的多樣化目標和限制所驅動。<lb/>單一的綜合分數往往掩蓋了模型在不同提示類型和組合下的行為。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們對 LMArena（前身為 Chatbot Arena）基準中使用的數據集進行了深入分析，並通過設計一個互動可視化界面作為設計探針來調查這一評估挑戰。<lb/>我們的分析顯示，數據集在某些主題上有很大的偏斜，模型排名在不同的提示切片中有所變化，並且基於偏好的判斷以模糊其預期範圍的方式使用。<lb/>基於這一分析，我們介紹了一個可視化界面，允許用戶通過選擇和加權提示切片來定義自己的評估優先事項，並探索排名如何相應變化。<lb/>一項定性研究表明，這種互動方法提高了透明度，並支持更具上下文特定的模型評估，指向設計和使用 LLM 排行榜的替代方式。
2604.21769.author: Minji Jung et.al.
2604.21769.authors: Minji Jung, Minjae Lee, Yejin Kim, Sarang Choi, Minsuk Kahng
